response.justification: The candidate is a strong and versatile senior engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His career centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Ember.js), while professional C++ experience is limited to a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his skills list. The role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise plus industrial automation, MQTT/IoT protocols, and IEC 62443 secure development experience, none of which are evidenced. His DLang low-latency systems work shows relevant systems-programming instincts, but not enough to carry a senior C++ architecture position in industrial cybersecurity.
strengths: Genuine low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) from Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong engineering craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, and active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot background spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile, indicating fast adaptability
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, a decade ago) for a role requiring expert-level C++/STL and multi-threaded architecture
No exposure to industrial automation, MQTT, OPC UA/Modbus, or IEC 62443 SDL practices central to the role
Recent career trajectory (full-stack web, AI tooling) points away from the on-prem industrial C++ domain, raising fit and retention doubts
